summary_text: 1) **Findings (ordered by severity)** - **Critical: stuck `posting` rows can become unrecoverable because recovery reads local cache before Notion and only scans local `status=="posting"`** - Recovery is executed from local queue first: [scheduler.py#L421](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L421), [scheduler.py#L423](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L423). - Recovery only processes posts whose local status is `posting`: [notion_sync.py#L191](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L191). - CAS lock sets Notion to `posting`, but local status is not set to `posting` before publish: [notion_sync.py#L143](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L143), [scheduler.py#L564](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L564). - If posting fails after lock acquisition, lock cleanup relies on recovery; current flow can miss it indefinitely. - **High: schedule mutations are not durable under sync failure/race; local schedule edits can be silently dropped** - Scheduler intentionally preserves only dirty `status`/`force_post`, not dirty `scheduled_for`: [scheduler.py#L456](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L456), [scheduler.py#L459](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L459). - Merge path explicitly clears `_schedule_changed_at`: [notion_sync.py#L537](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L537). - Backfill creates local schedule dirty flags: [post_generator.py#L446](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/post_generator.py#L446), [post_generator.py#L447](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/post_generator.py#L447). - If Notion push fails once after backfill, next cycle can reload old schedule from Notion and discard local pending schedule mutation. - **High: state machine is not enforced; unknown statuses can be written** - Schema defines allowed statuses only: [schema.py#L11](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/schema.py#L11). - Validation is warning-only, not rejecting: [schema.py#L66](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/schema.py#L66), [schema.py#L83](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/schema.py#L83). - Generator writes `notion_sync_failed` (not in enum): [auto_generator.py#L556](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/auto_generator.py#L556). - **Medium-High: `apply_post_package` does blind full-property PATCH without optimistic concurrency** - Upsert patches full mapped properties in one shot: [apply_post_package.py#L133](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scripts/apply_post_package.py#L133), [apply_post_package.py#L138](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scripts/apply_post_package.py#L138). - No `last_edited_time` check here; this can overwrite operator edits (status/schedule/content) made in Notion between read and apply. - **Medium: `pull_notion_diff()` is non-paginated and may miss rows (>100)** - Single query call, no `has_more` handling: [notion_sync.py#L478](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L478), [notion_sync.py#L492](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L492). - `load_posts_from_notion()` is paginated; behavior mismatch increases reconciliation drift risk: [notion_sync.py#L575](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L575), [notion_sync.py#L586](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L586). - **Medium: Notion SoT is fail-open for posting path** - On Notion load failure scheduler falls back to local and can still post: [scheduler.py#L436](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L436), [scheduler.py#L468](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L468). - This conflicts with strict “Notion is authoritative” policy during outages. - **Low: view-vs-page consistency is architecturally correct** - Runtime reads DB/page APIs directly and does not depend on Notion view IDs: [notion_sync.py#L562](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L562), [notion_sync.py#L630](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L630). - UI stale-view issues are presentation-side, not data-path-side. 2) **Recommended structural fixes** - Introduce a **real transition guard** (`transition(from,to,reason)`) and reject unknown statuses at write time. - Move to **durable mutation intents** for op...
